results.name: Palantir Technologies

results.summary: The overwhelming majority of $PLTR posts in this sample are spam, bot-driven WhatsApp group promotions, and NVDA-tagged noise with no substantive PLTR-specific content, severely limiting signal quality. Among the few genuine posts, sentiment skews mildly bullish with multiple contributors citing PLTR as undervalued relative to intrinsic price targets and including it in long-term compounding or generational wealth portfolios, though one post flags continued narrative sensitivity as a risk.

results.name: Iris Energy
keyThemes: Anthropic partnership speculation / Australia data center
Power capacity scarcity as competitive moat (6GW secured)
Timing mismatch concerns (2028 energization vs 2027 Anthropic deadline)
GPU/AI infrastructure pivot from Bitcoin mining
Technical support levels and chart analysis
results.topBullish:
Leaked Anthropic docs reveal plans to buy 1.4GW of Aussie DC capacity -AFR
$IREN just announced on June 3rd its first-ever project in Australia: 800 MW AI data center campus in Bundey, South Australia.  Is Anthropic and IREN deal finally coming?
results.topBearish: Don't think it will be $IREN - Their energisation starts 2028, one year after Anthropic's 1GW deadline.
results.summary: Sentiment on $IREN is mixed but slightly bullish, dominated by speculation around a potential Anthropic partnership tied to leaked Australian data center expansion plans, though a recurring and credible bear thesis centers on a critical timing mismatch — IREN's Australian site isn't energized until 2028, one year after Anthropic's reported 2027 deadline. Bulls highlight IREN's 6GW secured power capacity as a scarce, durable moat in the AI infrastructure buildout, while bears and skeptics question execution competence and flag potential coordinated rumor pumping, keeping conviction levels divided.
